content:
This field is no longer reference counted, so it cannot be used as WorkItem::Pointer. The trait cannot be implemented for drm::device::Device<T> or ARef<drm::device::Device<T>> either, because of the orphan rule. This leads to two issues:

a) We now have to build an extra indirection just to make this work, i.e.: we have to place inner members of drm::device::Device::data behind an Arc<T> just to share them with the worker, even though ARef<drm::device::Device<T>> is already ref-counted.

b) We now have to group fields into the same struct to place them in said Arc. This is just so that they can be accessed by the worker, even though they may be unrelated otherwise, i.e.:

struct DriverData {
  // this is clunky and unnecessary
  shared_worker_fields: Arc<SharedDriverData>,
  ...
}

c) different workers may need to access different fields, so eventually lots of things will end up in the SharedDriverData struct of the previous example

This looks like a regression (feature-wise) from what we had before.

cc @Danilo Krummrich @Alice Ryhl
hmm, I guess I was too eager to comment here, I now see there is nothing forbidding callers from setting Device<T>::data to an Arc<T>, which would yield the same behavior as before apparently

I don't see how this is different than before, if you really want drm::Device::data to be reference counted you can do that:

impl drm::Driver for MyDriver {
   type Data = Arc<MyData>;
   [...]
}
However, this seems like a workaround. Usually, you want your data to live exactly as long as the drm::Device, hence no need for a separate allocation.
Maybe workqueue should work slightly different and take a ForeignOwnable instead.
yeah, we now have two refcounts for something that should have the same lifetime, true
I'm trying to see your point here with the ForeignOwnable by the way

I don't know exactly how the workqueue stuff works (would need to take a look), but it seemed that you were implying that you can only implement it on your own type, hence wouldn't struct WqData(ARef<drm::Device>) work?
I think we can just implement it for Device and have it dispatch to the inner T
